name: Homemade Slice & Bake Peanut Butter Cookies
recipeIngredient: 2 cups peanut butter, creamy or chunky
recipeInstructions:
1. Cut four 14-inch x 12-inch pieces of waxed paper or plastic wrap. Set aside.
2. In a large bowl, cream the vegetable shortening with the granulated and brown sugars and peanut butter. Beat in vanilla extract and eggs until light and fluffy.
3. In another bowl, combine flour and baking soda. Stir the flour mixture into the egg and butter mixture; blend well.
4. Stir in chocolate chips and nuts; stir to distribute evenly.
5. Divide the dough into 4 pieces. Shape each piece into an 8-inch - 10-inch roll.
6. Wrap rolls in waxed paper. Place the rolls in a rectangular freezer container with a tight-fitting lid, or into a large freezer zip-lock bag, or wrap well in heavy-duty aluminum foil. Label with date and contents.
7. Store in freezer. Use within 6 months.
To prepare cookies:
1. Slightly thaw one roll of peanut butter cookie dough.
2. Preheat oven to 350°F.
3. Cut the dough into 1-inch thick slices; cut each slice into quarters and roll each quarter into a ball.
4. Arrange balls on ungreased cookie sheets, placing about 1-1/2 inches apart.
5. Flatten the dough balls with the tines of a fork in the traditional criss-cross pattern.
6. Bake for about 8-10 minutes, or until browned around the edges. Remove from oven and cool on wire racks.
7. Makes about 3 dozen cookies.
8. Makes 4 rolls of cookie dough, or enough for about 12 dozen cookies.
